title: GET /api/v1/statuses/:id — status serialization
description: Implement the status retrieval endpoint with full Mastodon Status object serialization. The Status object has many required fields — match mastodon.social's shape exactly. Key invariants: id is a string, media_attachments is always an array (never null), mentions/tags/emojis are always arrays, in_reply_to_id is string or null (never empty string), reblog is null for normal posts.
design: Status.url = https://{domain}/@{username}/{id}. Status.uri = ap_id. Status.content = content_html (not raw markdown). Status.account: full Account object embedded. Status.application: the OAuth app the post was through, or {'name':'Web','website':null}. favourites_count and reblogs_count from DB counts.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/statuses/:id returns correct Status JSON; all required fields present; id is a string; media_attachments is [] not null; in_reply_to_id is null not '' for non-replies; Status.application is {'name':'Web','website':null} for CLI-posted statuses; visibility is one of public/unlisted/private/direct
